text.title: Typed Handlers, Service Layer, Concurrent Processing
text.summary: Axum typed extractor pattern, service layer with tokio::try_join! for concurrent DB calls, and Semaphore-bounded batch processing.

rule.statement: Extract all handler inputs via typed extractors (Path, Query, Json, State); return Result<Json<T>, AppError> — never raw StatusCode + String.
rule.rationale: Typed extractors fail at the framework boundary with a 422 before business logic runs. AppError with IntoResponse keeps error shape consistent across all routes.

rule.statement: Use tokio::try_join! to run independent DB queries in parallel; use tokio::task::spawn_blocking for CPU-intensive work like password hashing.
rule.rationale: Sequential awaits on independent queries serialise IO unnecessarily. CPU work (Argon2) blocks the async executor thread — spawn_blocking offloads it to a dedicated pool.

rule.statement: Bound concurrency with a Semaphore when processing a collection of async tasks; use buffer_unordered to collect results preserving order semantics.
rule.rationale: Unbounded spawning can exhaust DB connection pools or file descriptors. Semaphore + buffer_unordered processes at most N concurrent tasks without blocking the executor.
